---
Neither of these is a Fable task, which is the tell that the Fable session did its job: it produced the generative slice, and what is left is mechanical harvest plus a code build. Harvest routes to Sonnet, the engine build to Opus. They are independent (the engine uses the two fixtures you already have, not the withheld security cluster), so run them in parallel.  ## Task A: harvest the five Willison bodies. Sonnet 5, medium effort.  Mechanical fetch-and-deposit. Willison's blog is open, so no 403 risk. This resolves the data gap only; authoring the now-classifiable security-cluster delta is a separate generative pass (see the reveal at the end).  ``` Harvest the five June 2026 Simon Willison security-cluster post bodies into the Cast corpus so the withheld shift-delta candidate can classify next cycle. Sonnet 5, medium effort. This is a HARVEST task only. Do NOT author a delta record (that is a separate generative pass).  READ FIRST:   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/CLAUDE.md  (HARVEST stage conventions, venv, scripts)   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/farm/corpus/simon-willison/deltas/delta-2026-07-02-simon-willison.md     (the record that flagged the withheld cluster and named the five posts)   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/farm/corpus/simon-willison/sources.yaml   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/farm/corpus/simon-willison/processing-log.md  THE FIVE POSTS (June 2026 security cluster, currently RSS-title-only in corpus):   role confusion; 2,000-hackers red-team; CVE-2026-LGTM; AI and Liability; AI Compass.   Source: simonwillison.net (open blog). Resolve each title to its real post URL, fetch the   FULL body, not the RSS title.  DEPOSIT (Cast HARVEST discipline): - Write each full body to the simon-willison corpus as content, dated to the real publish date. - Update sources.yaml: URL, publish date, and fetch_status per post. Grade fetch_status for   QUOTE ELIGIBILITY (an engine-relevant finding from the prior session): full-body fetched =   quotable; anything that stays title/summary-only = not-quotable. Mark these five as   full-body/quotable once fetched. - Append a processing-log entry describing the harvest.  NO FABRICATION: if any post 403s or is genuinely unavailable, flag NEEDS-BRIEN with the exact URL and leave it not-quotable. Never invent body text.  GATES / DISCIPLINE: - Use the Cast venv: /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/.venv/bin/python , never bare python3. - Run validate_yaml.py if you touch registry; run chain_audit.py after and confirm zero new violations. - L4 reversible local. Commit in the cast repo, specific files only, conventional message,   Co-Authored-By trailer. Never git add -A. No external comms.  CLOSE-OUT (honest): this resolves the DATA gap only. State that the security-cluster delta is now classification-ready and that AUTHORING that delta is the downstream step, not done here. Note in the signal whether all five landed full-body or any are still NEEDS-BRIEN. ```  ## Task B: build the shift-delta engine. Opus 4.8, high effort, TDD.  This is the real code build, with genuine design decisions (diff baseline, schema enforcement, cross-voice automation, the Pulse seam). The two fixtures become the first tests, which makes TDD the natural spine.  ``` Build the shift-delta engine that productizes the hand-authored capability. Opus 4.8, high effort. Use test-driven development (superpowers:test-driven-development): the two existing fixture delta records are your first acceptance tests.  READ FIRST (in order):   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/crucible/spec/2026-07-06-shift-delta-capability.md   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/crucible/.intent/decisions.md   (CR-004 placement decision)   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/crucible/.intent/signals/SIG-2026-07-10-shift-delta-first-divergence-records.md     (close-out from the manual pass: the three engine-relevant findings live here)   the two FIXTURES (the engine must reproduce/validate these exactly):     /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/farm/corpus/simon-willison/deltas/delta-2026-07-02-simon-willison.md     /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/farm/corpus/ed-zitron/deltas/delta-2026-07-10-ed-zitron--simon-willison.md   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/crucible/engine/synthesis-method.md   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/crucible/contracts/crucible-cast-port.md   /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/pulse/src/shifts.py   (the arc layer this surfaces above)  SCOPE (the "not claimed" list from the manual pass, now the build): - Diff logic: diff each freshening cycle against the voice's ESTABLISHED ARC as baseline. Honor   the finding: baseline is the arc, NOT the latest synthesis. The true first-cycle window is the   May/June 2026 material (the spec's Glasswing / autonomous-influence worked-examples are already   inside the arc's inflection table, so do not treat them as new). - Delta schema + enforcement: taxonomy new-position | reversal | escalation | extension |   restatement. Restatement is recorded and suppressed from the surface (auditable "nothing moved"). - The two-quote guard: a passage needs two dated quotes to classify; grade eligibility from   fetch_status (title/summary-only = not quotable, blocks classification, flag needs-harvest). - Cross-voice automation per CR-004: host the record under the alphabetical-first slug, carry a   voices list and sibling pointers, preserve both trajectories, emit a pointer not a verdict. - Pulse shift-channel wiring: surface deltas alongside the recency digest and the arc index. This   is an INTERNAL surface only. Do NOT wire it to publish anywhere external.  PLACEMENT (a real decision, record it): Per CR-002 (method-ownership-now / physical-extraction-deferred), the other Crucible-owned scripts physically live in /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/engine/scripts/ . Decide whether the shift-delta engine co-locates there or warrants a new module under /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/crucible/engine/ , per the operating charter, and record the choice as CR-005 in the Crucible decisions file with the rationale.  CATCH-NET: extend chain_audit.py with a delta-record invariant (schema + taxonomy validity + two-quote guard) so a malformed delta is caught by the shared S3 audit, not just by tests.  GATES / DISCIPLINE: - Cast venv for all Python: /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/cast/.venv/bin/python . - Tests green + chain_audit clean (zero new violations) before you call it built. The two fixtures   are regression tests: the engine must classify them the same way the hand pass did. - L4 reversible local. Commit in the OWNING repo per file (cast repo for cast/engine + farm,   crucible repo for crucible spec/decisions), specific files only, conventional message,   Co-Authored-By trailer. Never git add -A. No external comms.  CLOSE-OUT (honest closure-DoD): this moves the capability from DEMONSTRATED to PRODUCTIZED. State upstream_control_path (the engine module), catch_mechanism (the new chain_audit invariant + the fixture regression tests), pipeline_survival (survives the nightly freshening run; fixtures guard regression). If any piece stays manual (e.g. cross-voice pair selection), say so plainly rather than claiming full automation. ```  Two riders, since your Fable window closes in about 3 days:  - Once Task A lands the five bodies, the withheld security-cluster delta becomes authorable, and authoring it is the generative act Fable is best at. If you want it on Fable, run a short Fable delta pass on the simon-willison security cluster right after harvest completes, before the window closes. It is small (one voice, one cycle, now with real bodies) and it gives you a third fixture. I would sequence it Sonnet harvest, then Fable delta, rather than wait for the Opus engine. - The side-signal the session left (ed-zitron last_synthesized lags its corpus by four passes) is a separate cheap Sonnet re-extract, not part of either task above. I can fold it into the Task A harvest prompt as a rider so one Sonnet session clears both, which is the more economical path.  I recommend folding the Zitron re-extract into Task A now so you spend one Sonnet session instead of two. Say the word is the wrong framing here: it is reversible local cleanup, so unless you object, add it to the harvest prompt and run A and B in parallel, with the Fable security-cluster delta slotted between A and the engine build.
